title: Keeping Company With Sorrow
intention: To sit beside grief with tenderness, letting it be felt without being fixed.

lines.text: Welcome. You don't have to be anywhere but here.
Let your body settle into whatever is holding you.
There is nothing to mend in this hour. Only company to keep.
Let your eyes close, or rest them low and soft on the floor.
Feel the weight of you arriving, like dusk arrives. Slowly. Without asking.
Take one breath that belongs only to you.
And let it leave you without being chased.
Notice the air at the edge of your nose, cool coming in.
Warmer going out, carrying a little of you with it.
Somewhere in you there is a sorrow. You already know its name.
You don't have to speak it. You don't have to move it.
Just let yourself know where it lives in the body.
Maybe the throat. Maybe the chest, that low quiet room.
Maybe the belly, or the hollow behind the eyes.
Wherever it is, let your breath go and stand near it.
Not to push. Not to lift it away.
Only to say, quietly, I see you. You may stay.
Grief is not a problem the breath has come to solve.
It is love that has nowhere left to go, so it stays close.
Let it be heavy if it is heavy. You are strong enough to be tired.
Breathe toward the ache, the way you'd carry a candle into a dim room.
Not to chase the dark out. Only so the dark has company.
If tears come, let them come. They are not a failure of calm.
If nothing comes, that is welcome too.
You are allowed to feel exactly as much as you feel.
No more, to be brave. No less, to be spared.
Let one hand rest where the sorrow lives, if that feels kind.
Warmth against it. The simple weight of being held.
Breathe here a while. I'll stay with you.
Nothing is being fixed. Nothing needs to be.
You are simply a person, sitting beside their own tenderness.
When you're ready, let your breath grow a little fuller.
Carry this gentleness with you, like a small light cupped in the hand.
Whatever you feel as you go, you are not alone in it. Rest now.
